LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LYNNE CRAWFORD'S MOTION TO APPOINT DON SULLIVAN AS SPECIAL MASTER IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITIONS THAT: 1) UNLESS AND UNTIL THIS COURT MAKES A DETERMINATION THAT DEFENDANT FRANK'S CAULKING & WATERPROOFING HAS THE ABILITY TO PAY THE FEES ASSESSED BY THE SPECIAL MASTER, FRANK'S CAULKING IS NOT REQUIRED TO PAY ANY SPECIAL MASTER FEES; 2) THE SPECIAL MASTER FEES ARE TO BE ASSESSED ONLY AS TO MS. CRAWFORD AND ALL OTHER DEFENDANTS WHO HAVE APPEARED IN THIS CASE AND RECEIVED NOTICE OF THIS MOTION; AND 3) ANY DEFENDANTS WHO APPEAR IN THIS CASE HEREAFTER ARE ONLY OBLIGATED TO PAY THE SPECIAL MASTER FEES IF THEY AGREE TO DO SO OR THERE IS A COURT ORDER EXPLICITLY REQUIRING THEM TO DO SO. MS. CRAWFORD HAS SHOWN AMPLE GOOD CAUSE FOR THE APPOINTMENT OF A SPECIAL MASTER AND THE ONLY OBJECTION TO THE APPOINTMENT OF A SPECIAL MASTER IS BY FRANK'S CAULKING BASED ON ASSERTED FINANCIAL HARDSHIP, WHICH HAS BEEN ADDRESSED BY THE FIRST CONDITION. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Timothy Lis Dba As Lis Construction
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ron Lunski, Esq. 415-426-3525 of Lunski Law for Plaintiff.
Allison Harvey, Esq. 530-217-3520 of A.L. Harvey Law PLC for Defendant TIMOTHY LIS DBA LIS CONSTRUCTION through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT TIMOTHY LIS DBA LIS CONSTRUCTION's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Timothy Lis Dba As Lis Construction.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling . Defendant Timothy Lis' motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default judgment and default are to be vacated.

The default entered against Timothy Lis on June 25, 2018 is vacated on the condition that must be accepted by Timothy Lis that he will not assert any argument that any claims of plaintiff Lynne Crawford against him are barred by the five year statute. Timothy Lis has shown good cause to set aside the June 25, 2018 based on surprise, inadvertence, mistake and excusable neglect per CCP 473(b) due to his mistaken belief that his brother and general contractor on the project would handle his defense for him without need for him to take action. Vacating the June 25, 2018 default also comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, especially where, as here, plaintiff Lynne Crawford has not identified any legal prejudice to her if the motion is granted other than the possible application of the five year statute.

In addition, an answer needs to be filed within 10 days; no other responsive pleading.

Counsel for Timothy Lis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, delete language on the efficacy of the default in compliance with CRC 3.1312. (as fully reported on the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:

Andrew D. White, of Murtaugh Meyer Nelson & Treglia LLP, Tel. 949.794.4000, for x-deft. KM Drywall, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. PLAINTIFF LYNNE CRAWFORD Motion To Appoint Don Sullivan As Special Master is granted on the conditions that: 1) unless and until this court makes a determination that defendant Frank's Caulking & Waterproofing has the ability to pay the fees assessed by the special master, Frank's Caulking is not required to pay any special master fees; 2) the special master fees are to be assessed only as to Ms. Crawford and all other defendants who have appeared in this case and received notice of this motion; and 3) any defendants who appear in this case hereafter are only obligated to pay the special master fees if they agree to do so or there is a court order explicitly requiring them to do so. Ms. Crawford has shown ample good cause for the appointment of a special master and the only objection to the appointment of a special master is by Frank's Caulking based on asserted financial hardship, which has been addressed by the first condition. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Motion To Compel Compliance With Contractual Dispute Resolution Provisions, And Motion To Stay Pending Litigation
Off Calendar Mar-20-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. QUINLAN S. TOM OF MCINERNEY & DILLON, TEL. NO. (510) 465-7100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CRYSTAL GAUDETTE OF KENNEDY, ARCHER & GIFFEN, TEL. NO. (831) 373-7500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT RJL CONSTRUCTION;
ATTY. DAVID L. ANDERSEN OF THE ANDERSEN AND ZIMMER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 835-4952 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT KAUFMAN;
ATTY. DAVID A. KULISCH OF THE RANDALL DANSKIN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (509) 747-2052 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT TANGLEFOOT MASTER BUILDERS INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT J. LIS' Motion To Compel Compliance With Contractual Dispute Resolution Provisions, And Motion To Stay Pending Litigation - Motion to Compel Arbitration is denied. Under CCP 1281.2, a court may deny a motion to compel arbitration when a party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action with a third party that arises out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and presents a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. Henry v. Alcove Inv., Inc., (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 94. Plaintiff has not established that Defendant has waived his right to compel arbitration. However, because allegations for negligence against all Defendants stem from the same series of events and Defendant Kaufman is not subject to binding arbitration the court denies the motion to compel arbitration. Balancing the interests of Plaintiff, Defendant Robert J. Lis, and Defendant Tanglefoot Master Builders, who have signed a contractual arbitration agreement, with Brian Kaufman, who has not signed a contractual arbitration agreement, the court finds in favor of denying the petition to compel arbitration and stay pending litigation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/ehg
